title: How Much Does a Fractional CMO Cost? | Daniel Fox
meta_description: What a fractional CMO costs: most run $8,000 to $22,000 a month; mine start at $10,000. Why the rate is the wrong first question, and how to weigh it against a full-time hire and the agency spend you already carry. Daniel Fox, fractional B2C CMO.

mainEntity.name: How much does a fractional CMO cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on scope, but here is the anchor: most fractional CMOs charge a monthly retainer between $8,000 and $22,000, and my engagements start at $10,000 a month, scoped and sized to the work rather than sold as a fixed package. The more useful question than the rate is what the seat returns. For an established company selling a considered, high-ticket purchase, a single incremental customer can cover months of the fee, and the relevant comparison is the retainer against the true cost of a full-time CMO, which runs $300,000 to $450,000 a year all in before a 25 to 35 percent search fee and a six-month ramp, and against the agency and freelancer spend you are probably already carrying with no senior leader deciding whether it is the right work. Daniel Fox is a fractional B2C CMO whose engagements start at $10,000 a month.
